title: ADR: Using Architecture Decision Records
description: An explanation of what Architecture Decision Records are and how we use them
content:
ADR: Using Architecture Decision Records

Context

As our UI component library grows, we need a structured way to document
important architectural decisions. This ensures that:

- Team members understand the context and reasoning behind important decisions
- Future contributors can reference past decisions rather than revisiting
  settled issues
- We maintain a useful historical record of how the project has evolved

Decision

We will use Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) to document signific

title: Breakpoints
description: The list of available breakpoints
content:
Breakpoints

Breakpoints define specific screen widths at which your design should adapt to
provide an optimal viewing experience for users. They are thresholds that
represent a starting point for changes in styling, such as font sizes, layouts,
and visibility of elements.

Each breakpoint is a minimum width, meaning that styles associated with that
breakpoint apply to screen sizes equal to or larger than the specified width.
The styles remain active until the next breakpoint threshold is reache

title: Colors
description: available colors
content:
Colors

Our color system is built on Radix Colors,
which organizes colors into scales based on contrast levels to ensure
clarity and usability across various contexts. Each scale provides a range
of tones, from subtle backgrounds to high-contrast elements like text and
interactive borders. A full scale for a given color is referred to as a
colorPalette (e.g., colorPalette: "red").

The scales are _interchangeable_, meaning a color like red can be swapped with
green without losing the intended co

title: ADR: Nimbus Software Lifecycle States
description: Architecture decision record defining the lifecycle states for Nimbus components, hooks, utilities, classes, etc.
content:
ADR: Nimbus Software Lifecycle States

Context

As Nimbus grows and evolves, we need a clear system to communicate the
stability, support level, and future trajectory of our components, hooks,
utilities, functions, and classes to consumers. Without a standardized
lifecycle system, consumers cannot make informed decisions about adoption,
migration planning, or production readiness.

The lack of clear lifecycle states leads to:
- Uncertainty about which features are safe for production use
- Diffi

content:

Badges are small, visually distinct labels used to highlight status, category,
or counts associated with other UI elements. They feature concise text and
customizable styling for clear communication without overwhelming the primary
content.

Medium

This is the default size.

title: Box
description: Box is a basic layout component that serves as a wrapper or container. It's based on the Chakra UI Box component and supports all style props for rapid UI development.
content:
Import

``jsx
import { Box } from "@commercetools/nimbus";
`

Usage

Basic usage

`jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Box p="100" bg="primary.3">
    Basic Box
  </Box>
);
`

As a flex container

`jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Box display="flex" gap="100">
    <Box p="100" bg="primary.3">
      Item 1
    </Box>
    <Box p="100" bg="primary.3">
      Item 2
    </Box>
  </Box>
);
`

With border

`jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Box p="100" border="solid-50" borderColor="primary.3">
    Box with b

title: Button

content:

Buttons are clickable elements that initiate actions. They're how we encourage
users to take action and explore different parts of our interface.

Primary actions

Primary colorPalette, filled style, medium size. For the principal call to action on the
page. Primary buttons should only appear once per screen.

title: Card
description: A versatile container component presents self-contained information, often combining text, images, and actions, making it ideal for displaying summaries or content previews.
content:
Overview

This versatile container component often integrates text, images, and actions.
Interaction methods differ: some are clickable, others use buttons and links,
and some are purely informational, grouping content without direct interaction.

Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

Figma library

description: Checkbox allows for toggling of features, allowing users to select more than one option where needed. Checkboxes support selected, unselected, indeterminate, and disabled states.
content:
Overview

Checkboxes are commonly used in forms, settings panels, and other interactive
contexts where users can choose one or more options from a set. See below
variables for further choices.

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

Figma library

Get familiar with the features.

Selection

Unchecked

Represents the default or unchosen state.

`

Visually confirms a user’s

title: Code

content: Add description later.

title: Collapsible motion
description: A modern, accessible implementation of collapsible content with smooth height animations. Built with React Aria for accessibility and Chakra UI for consistent theming.
content:
Overview

CollapsibleMotion is a compound component that provides smooth, accessible
collapsible content with height animations. It combines React Aria's
accessibility features with Chakra UI's theming system to deliver a robust
foundation for disclosure patterns, accordions, and expandable content sections.

Key features

- Smooth height animations: Customizable duration with automatic content
  measurement
- Full accessibility support: Proper ARIA attributes and screen reader
  compatibility
-

title: Combo box
description: A combobox is an input that combines a text field with a pop-up list of options. It allows users to select a value from a predefined list or enter their own custom value.
content:
Overview

The combobox component provides both selection and text input capabilities. It
displays a list of options that users can choose from, while also allowing them
to type directly into the text field. This functionality improves user
experience by assisting with value selection and, in some cases, prevents
invalid entries.

Key features

- External State Management: Full control over async logic using React Stately's useAsyncList
- Debouncing: Use useDebouncedCallback from use-debounce to

title: Data Display
description: Components for presenting data or visual content.
content:
Data Display

Components for presenting data or visual content.

<CategoryOverview variant="list" />

title: Data table
description: Data tables display sets of data across rows and columns. Use them to organize information, enable comparisons, and allow users to inspect and interact with large datasets.
content:
Overview

Data tables are essential tools for presenting sets of data in a structured
format, utilizing rows and columns to enhance clarity. They serve multiple
purposes: organizing information systematically, facilitating comparisons
between different data points, and providing users with the ability to
thoroughly inspect and interact with extensive datasets. By incorporating
features such as sorting, filtering, and pagination, data tables empower users
to navigate through large volumes of info

title: Date input
description: A date input component allows users to enter a specific date.
content:
Overview

The date input field uses a conventional date format, such as mm/dd/yyyy, which
is localized based on the user's region. Users can type a date directly into the
field. The component automatically formats the input as the user types.

Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

Figma Link
Storybook Link

content:
Overview

Date picker can be simple or complex, allowing users to select a single date and
or specific times. There are two offered sizes, the default being medium for
most use cases.

Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

`

title: Date range picker
description: Allows users to select a start and end date from a calendar interface.
content:
Overview

Date range pickers allow users to select a range of dates. The component
includes two date input fields for start and end dates, and a calendar that
appears on focus. This component uses a single input field to contain both the
start and end dates. There are two offered sizes, the default being medium for
most use cases.

Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

Figma library

Variables

Get familiar with the features.

Visual options

Med

title: Dialog
description: A foundational dialog component for overlays that require user attention and interaction. Built with React Aria Components for accessibility and WCAG 2.1 AA compliance.
content:
Overview

Dialogs are overlay windows that appear on top of the main content to display
important information, gather user input, or require user decisions. They
temporarily disable the main interface until the user completes an action or
dismisses the dialog.

Key features

- Accessibility First: Built with React Aria Components for WCAG 2.1 AA
  compliance
- Flexible Positioning: Support for center, top, and bottom placements
- Responsive Sizing: Multiple size variants from xs to full-screen
-

title: Draggable list
description: The draggable list allows users to easily reorder or manage a sequence of items via a direct drag-and-drop interaction.
content:
Overview

The draggable list component presents structured, ordered content where the
sequence itself is meaningful. It uses visual and functional cues to enable
users to physically manipulate the list order by dragging an item to a new
insertion point, providing immediate visual feedback throughout the process.

Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

Figma library
Storybook
React Aria Docs

Variables

Get familiar with the features.

Interactions

title: Drawer
description: A foundational drawer component for overlays that require user attention and interaction. Built with React Aria Components for accessibility and WCAG 2.1 AA compliance.
content:
Overview

Drawers are overlay windows that appear on top of the main content to display
important information, gather user input, or require user decisions. They
temporarily disable the main interface until the user completes an action or
dismisses the drawer.

Key features

- Accessibility First: Built with React Aria Components for WCAG 2.1 AA
  compliance
- Flexible Positioning: Support for left, right, top, and bottom placements
- Responsive Sizing: Multiple size variants from xs to full-scr

content:
Overview

The field errors component is a vital utility for user-friendly form validation,
responsible for displaying context-specific, localized error messages for
individual form fields. It ensures users receive clear, immediate feedback on
validation issues, maintaining a consistent and accessible experience.

title: Flex
description: Flex is a layout component that provides a convenient way to create flexible layouts based on CSS Flexbox. It's based on the Chakra UI Flex component and supports all style props for rapid UI development.
content:
Overview

The Flex component is essentially a Box component with display: flex applied
by default. It provides an intuitive API for creating flexible layouts and
supports all flexbox properties.

Key features

- Flexbox by default: Automatically applies display: flex
- Style props: Supports all Chakra UI style props for rapid development
- Responsive design: Easily create responsive layouts with responsive props
- Semantic HTML: Can render as any HTML element using the as prop

Resources

Learn

title: Form field
description: Form fields are the building blocks of user interaction, collecting essential data, facilitating actions, and enabling customization within digital interfaces.
content:
Overview

Composed of several elements, a label, optional helper icon, a text input. There
is also an optional helper text feature that presents at the bottom of the
field.

Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

Figma library

Get familiar with the features.

Label options

Simple

Label is used to describe the form.

title: Forms
description: Components for form validation, localization, and error handling.
content:
Forms

Components for form validation, localization, and error handling.

<CategoryOverview variant="list" />

title: Grid
description: The Grid Layout Component provides a flexible and responsive way to structure content using a two-dimensional grid system. It allows elements to be arranged in rows and columns, enabling dynamic and efficient layouts for different screen sizes. The component accepts all the chakra supported properties and Grid.Item as children elements.
content:
Basic usage

Use Grid → When you need fine control over layout, spanning, and custom grid
behaviors.

``jsx
import { Grid } from "@commercetools/nimbus";
`

To use the Grid layout, ensure to wrap the individual grid items inside of the
<Grid.Item> component

`jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Grid gap="100">
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box key="1" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        Item 1
      </Box>
    </Grid.Item>
    <Grid.Item>
      <Box key="1" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
        Item 2
      </Box>

title: Group
description: Groups related elements together for better organization and layout.
content:
Overview

The Group component provides a flexible way to group related UI elements with
consistent spacing and alignment. It's particularly useful for creating forms,
toolbars, and other collections of interactive elements.

Variables

Get familiar with the features.

Basic Usage

``jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Group>
    <Button>First</Button>
    <Button>Second</Button>
    <Button>Third</Button>
  </Group>
)
``

title: Heading
description: The heading component establishes typographic hierarchy throughout the product experience. It is crucial for organizing content, defining page structure, and improving content scan ability.
content:
Overview

Good headings are essential for organizing information and engaging users. They
clearly communicate the content's topic, making the page scannable and easier to
navigate. Effective headlines also improve accessibility for screen readers and
aid search engine optimization (SEO) by defining content relevance.

Resources

Deep dive on details and access design library.

Figma library

Variables

Get familiar with the features.

Typographic scale and hierarchy

These text styles are the re

content:

Nimbus uses the Material Design icon library and adds custom SVG icons.

description: Provides a compact and visually efficient way to represent actions, especially when space is limited or the action is universally understood.
content:
Overview

These specifications define the standard styles and variations for icon buttons
within our design system, covering different visual treatments for diverse use
cases.

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

Figma library

Hierarchy

Most common: Ghost

Ghost icon buttons are used most often, the represent familiar actions that
don't compete with a primary action on the page.

``jsx live
const App = () => {
  return (
    <IconButton varia

title: Icon toggle button
description: An interactive icon button that toggles between two binary states such as active and inactive.
content:
Overview

An icon toggle button is used similarly to a checkbox or a radio button, but
supports a smaller interactive component that can be used to give a binary
active state choice to users that can be used alone or in combination with other
choices. A toggle button or icon toggle button gives a user a new way of
visualizing the information in the interface. It does not typically perform an
action. Please use a button or an icon button for these uses.

Resources

Deep dive into implementation d

title: Image
description: An image component is used to display visual content like photographs, illustrations, or graphics within the UI to support, illustrate, or enhance textual information.
content:
Overview

The image component offers core features designed for optimal performance,
accessibility, and responsiveness when displaying visual content.

Basic functions

``jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Image
    src="/images/image/image-sample.png"
    alt="Example image showing basic functionality"
    width="100%"
    maxWidth="9600"
  />
)
`

- Source set (srcSet) and sizes: This allows the component to define
  multiple image sources at different resolutions, enabling the browser to
  autom

title: Inline svg
description: The InlineSvg component allows you to render arbitrary SVG markup as an icon with built-in XSS protection. It sanitizes the provided SVG string to remove potentially dangerous elements and attributes before rendering.
content:
Key features

- Security First: Automatic sanitization of SVG content to prevent XSS
  attacks
- API Compatible: Drop-in replacement for existing InlineSvg implementations
- Accessibility: Support for title and description elements
- Consistent Styling: Uses the same recipe system as the Icon component
- Performance: Memoized sanitization for optimal re-renders
- Zero External Dependencies: Uses native browser APIs for parsing and
  sanitization

Basic usage

``jsx live
const App = () => {
  con

content:
Overview

The link is flexible to many uses and matching text styles for common
placements. Most commonly used in dialog boxes, alerts, and within body copy.

title: List
description: The list component organizes related items or data into a vertical, scannable format.
content:
Overview

The list component presents structured content which can include details such as
text, icons, and actions, in a vertical arrangement. With improved readability
letting readers quickly scan the content and compare relevant details.

Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

Vertical lists

title: Loading spinner
description: A circular visual indicator that displays the loading status of an ongoing process. It can represent either determinate or indeterminate progress.
content:
Overview

A loading spinner's primary usage is to visually communicate to the user that a
process is ongoing and that they need to wait. It indicates that the system is
working and prevents the user from thinking the application is frozen or
unresponsive.

Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

Figma library

Variables

Get familiar with the features.

Visuals

On white background

Most common, this is the default spinner animation to be used on w

title: Localized field
description: A localized field is a single component that enables users to input and manage content for multiple language versions of a piece of data within one unified form area.
content:
Overview

The localized field component serves as a container that displays and manages
content across various defined locales. It minimizes clutter by allowing users
to toggle between language versions using a control while ensuring that the data
for each specified locale is correctly captured, validated, and stored in the
application.

Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

Figma library
Storybook

Variables

Get familiar with the features.

Siz

title: Menu
description: A temporary surface that displays a list of choices or actions. It typically appears when a user interacts with an element like a button or icon, or performs a specific action.
content:
Overview

Menus present short, actionable items in a vertical list, ensuring easy
navigation and selection. They display below the trigger with a 8px space. To
close a menu, a user makes a selection, or in the case several selections may be
made, after a confirm button or a click off the active area.

Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

React ARIA
ARIA Pattern
Figma library

title: Money input
description: A controlled input for money values that combines currency selection with amount input, supporting both standard and high-precision monetary values.
content:
Overview

The money input component combines a currency selector (a dropdown menu) and a
numerical input field into a single, cohesive unit, offering robust states like
default, invalid, and disabled, alongside specialized handling for focused and
hovered interactions.

Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

React Aria Docs

Get familiar with the features.

A multi-line text input allows users to enter multiple lines of text. It expands
vertically to accommodate more content.

title: Navigation
description: Components for moving through an application.
content:
Navigation

Components for navigating through an application.

<CategoryOverview variant="list" />

title: Nimbus i18n provider
description: The NimbusI18nProvider component provides internationalization (i18n) context for React Aria components in your Nimbus application. It controls how dates, numbers, and other locale-specific content is formatted across all React Aria-based components.
content:
Overview

NimbusI18nProvider is a proxy component that wraps React Aria's I18nProvider,
making it easy to set the locale for all React Aria components like DatePicker,
Calendar, and NumberInput. This ensures consistent formatting across your
application.

Key features

- Locale-aware formatting: Controls date, number, and currency formatting
  based on locale
- React Aria integration: Built specifically for React Aria components
- BCP47 locale codes: Supports standard language-region codes (e.g.

title: Nimbus provider
description: The NimbusProvider component is a required wrapper that provides theming, internationalization, and routing context for all Nimbus components. It must be placed at the root of your application.

title: Number input
description: A number input allows users to enter numerical values and adjust them incrementally.
content:
Overview

A number input allows users to enter a numeric value and adjust it using stepper
buttons. It provides a quick and precise way to modify values within defined
parameters, especially for small incremental changes.

Key features

- Value Clamping: If a user types a value outside the defined min or max
  range, the value will snap to the nearest valid step or the min/max value on
  blur.
- Decimals: Supports decimal values and step sizes.
- Percentages: Can be configured to display values

title: PageContent
description: A layout component that constrains page content width and arranges content in single or multi-column layouts. It consolidates multiple width-based containers into a single compound component with explicit column control.
content:
Overview

PageContent provides a CSS grid-based centering pattern with configurable
max-width constraints and column layouts. It supports three width variants
(wide, narrow, full) and three column configurations (single, equal split, and
2:1 ratio) with built-in responsive collapse for multi-column layouts.

Key features

- Width constraints: Three variants control the max-width of the content
  area
- Column layouts: Single, equal-split (1/1), and sidebar (2/1) column
  configurations
- Respons

title: Pagination
description: Pagination is a component that divides a large set of content or data into sequential pages, providing navigation controls to move between them.
content:
Overview

Pagination contains controls for navigating with dedicated buttons for moving to the next and previous pages, visible page numbering for direct access, and clear feedback indicating the user's current page position within the total set of results.

Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

Figma library

Variables

Get familiar with the features.

Visuals

Page size selection paired with jump to page

Use this combination for the most contr

title: Password input
description: A password input is a text field that hides entered characters for secure password entry.
content:
Overview

Password input guidelines focus on creating secure and user-friendly fields for
entering sensitive credentials, emphasizing visual obscuring of input, clear
labeling and validation, accessibility considerations, and best practices for
password visibility toggles and error handling.

Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

Figma library

Variables

Get familiar with the features.

Visual options

Medium

``jsx live

title: Progress bar
description: A linear visual indicator that displays the loading or completion state of an ongoing process. It can represent either determinate or indeterminate progress.
content:
Overview

A progress bar's primary usage is to visually communicate to the user that a
process is ongoing. If used in combination with a flow or tasks, a progress bar
can give a visual indicator of where they currently are in the process and
estimate how much is left to complete the task.

Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

Figma library

Variables

Get familiar with the features.

Visuals

On white background

Most common, progress bar is use

title: Radio input

Radio inputs ensure users can easily select one option from a small, visible set

content:
Overview

Rich text input is made of two parts, the input where users can add text, and a
toolbar. This toolbar has been arranged to suit user's needs for text editing.

Users can change type styles, toggle bold, italics, underline, strikethrough,

title: Scoped search input
description: Enables users to define where their search query is applied using a closely paired scope selector.
content:
Overview

The scoped search input combines an input field with a functional scope selector
(often a dropdown or button group), ensuring users can precisely target their
query to a specific data segment, such as searching only 'Products' or only
'Customers', yielding more accurate and relevant results.

Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

Figma library
React Aria SearchField

Variables

Get familiar with the features.

Size

There are two sizes,

title: Search input
description: Allows users to discover and filter content by entering text to help users find relevant information efficiently.
content:
Overview

Search inputs provide a dedicated text field for users to enter keywords or phrases to query and retrieve matching data or content. They are essential for navigating large amounts of information.

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

Select inputs have two main appearances, two sizes, and several states that will

ARIA APG: Listbox Pattern

Separators enhance clarity by visually breaking up content. They can be

title: Simple grid
description: SimpleGrid offers an intuitive way to easily create responsive grid layouts.
content:
Basic usage

Use SimpleGrid → When you need a quick, responsive, equal-sized grid (e.g.,
product listings, image galleries).

``jsx
import { SimpleGrid } from "@commercetools/nimbus";
`

`jsx live
const App = () => (
  <SimpleGrid gap="100">
    <Box key="1" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
      Item 1
    </Box>
    <Box key="2" p="400" bg="neutral.7">
      Item 2
    </Box>
  </SimpleGrid>
)
`

With columns

Use the columns prop to define the number of columns in the grid layout.

`jsx live

title: Spacer
description: A simple layout component that automatically fills available space in flex layouts using flexGrow 1.
content:
Overview

Spacer is a utility component designed to create flexible spacing between
elements in flex layouts. It automatically expands to fill any remaining space,
making it perfect for creating navigation bars, toolbars, and other layouts
where you need to push elements apart.

Key features

- Automatic Space Filling: Uses flexGrow: 1 to occupy all available space
- Works in Any Direction: Supports both horizontal and vertical flex layouts
- Flexible Styling: Can accept any Chakra UI Box props

title: Split button
description: A split button merges a main action with a dropdown for related, less common actions.
content:
Overview

A split button merges a main action that is immediate with a secondary dropdown
menu that holds related actions that are used less often. This component
provides quick access to essential functions while offering a complete list of
options, all while saving space.

Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

Figma library

Variables

Get familiar with the features.

Visuals

Solid

Most common styling of the split button. Try to keep only one

title: Stack
description: An easily customizable Stack component, re-exported from Chakra UI stack component, that provides a consistent and responsive layout structure across different products. Stacks allow you to effortlessly build flexible layouts with automatic distribution. You can arrange elements horizontally or vertically, adding spacing and/or separators between each item.
content:
Basic usage

``jsx
import { Stack } from "@commercetools/nimbus";
`

`jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack>
    {" "}
    <Box key="1" p="400" bg="primary.7">
      Item 1
    </Box>
    <Box key="1" p="400" bg="primary.7">
      Item 2
    </Box>
  </Stack>
);
`

With direction

Use the direction prop to change the layout direction. The default direction is
"column" (vertical). Set it to "row" for horizontal stacking.

`jsx live
const App = () => (
  <Stack direction="row">
    <Box key="1" p=

title: Steps
description: A progress indicator with built-in navigation that visually communicates the user's position within a multi-step process such as forms, wizards, and onboarding flows.
content:
Overview

The Steps component helps users understand where they are in a multi-step
process. It provides clear visual feedback about completed, current, and
upcoming steps, making complex workflows feel more manageable and predictable.

Key Features

- Three size variants: Extra small (xs), small (sm), and medium (md) to fit
  different interface densities
- Two orientations: Horizontal for wide layouts and vertical for sidebar or
  narrow contexts
- Flexible indicators: Numeric step numbers wit

title: Switch
description: A clear, visual toggle, allowing users to activate or deactivate a setting quickly.
content:
Overview

This switch is an interactive element that provides instant visual feedback
allowing users to toggle binary options.

Resources

- Figma

Variables

Get familiar with the features.

Medium (md)

const App = () => <Switch size="md">Label</Switch>;
`

Small (sm)

`

Labels

With label

It is always good practice to have a label paired with a switch so a user knows
what is being toggled. The label responds to b

title: Table
description: The Table component provides a straightforward way to display static, read-only data in a tabular format. It uses semantic HTML table elements and is designed for simple data presentation without interactive features like sorting, filtering, or selection.
content:
Related components

- DataTable Component - Upgrade to DataTable when you need sorting, filtering, selection, pagination, or other interactive features for complex data management
The DataTable component is built for complex, interactive data management. If you only need to display static content, Table is the simpler, lighter choice.

title: Tabs
description: Organizes content into distinct sections, enabling users to switch views within a single container.
content:
Overview

Tabs are a navigational component used to organize and switch between different
sections of content within the same contextual view, allowing users to access
multiple related pages or views without navigating away from the main screen.

Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

users to efficiently scan and understand multiple pieces of metadata or filter
criteria associated with a product, order, or entity in a compact, manageable
space.

Resources

Deep dive into implementation details and access the Nimbus design library.

Readable and consistent text styling is important to creating effective user

building blocks and provide more options for creation, and there are still ways

title: Toast
description: Displays brief, temporary notifications that confirm actions or provide status updates without interrupting the user's workflow.
content:
Overview

Toasts are lightweight, non-modal notifications that appear temporarily at the edge of the screen. They provide contextual feedback about completed actions, system status, or important updates while allowing users to continue their work uninterrupted. Unlike alerts or dialogs, toasts automatically dismiss after a few seconds and don't require user interaction unless an action is provided.

Key characteristics

- Non-intrusive: Appears without blocking content or requiring immediate att

title: Toggle button
description: An interactive button that toggles between two binary states such as active and inactive.
content:
Overview

A toggle button is used similarly to a checkbox or a radio button, but supports
a smaller interactive component that can be used to give a binary active state
choice to users that can be used alone or in combination with other choices. A
toggle button or toggle icon button gives a user a new way of visualizing the
information in the interface. It does not typically perform an action. Please
use a button or an icon button for these uses.

Resources

Deep dive into implementation details

description: A set of closely related, mutually exclusive or complementary actions that are important enough to be displayed directly in the interface for quick access.
content:

Button groups can be used several ways, with and without icons, and spanning
from two selections to several.

content:

A toolbar is a handy container that organizes a group of related buttons and

essentially, it gives users quick access to the most common actions they need
right now.

title: Visually hidden
description: Makes content accessible to screen readers but hides it visually.
content:
Basic usage

Just wrap the content you want to hide visually, but keep accessible to screen
readers, with the VisuallyHidden component.

``jsx
const App = () => <VisuallyHidden>I am VisuallyHidden!</VisuallyHidden>;
`

Focusable content

If the content you want to hide visually contains focusable elements, add the
isFocusable property to the VisuallyHidden component. The content then
becomes visible if the first focusable element inside is focused with the
_keyboard_.

`jsx live
const App = () =

title: ADR: Standardizing Compound Component Extraction to a `components` Directory
description: Architecture decision record on organizing compound component implementations
content:
ADR: Standardizing Compound Component Extraction to a components Directory

Context

To enhance the organization, maintainability, and scalability of our components
that utilize the compound component pattern, we are establishing a consistent
directory structure for their implementation. This ADR outlines the principle of
extracting the core root component and its associated compound sub-components
into a dedicated components subdirectory.

Decision

For any component (<ComponentName>) that foll

title: ADR: On Consumer Component API's
description: Architecture decision record on how to structure component APIs for flexibility and maintainability
content:
ADR: On Consumer Component API's

Context

We needed an Alert component to show informational or warning messages. The
component should allow:

- A title and description.
- An optional dismiss button.
- Additional buttons or controls.
- Spacing/styling overrides (e.g. px, py).

In early explorations, we tried to expose everything via props on the main
Alert—this seemed convenient but would quickly became unwieldy when
requirements changed. This led to a discussion about how to structure the API

Contributing

Contribute to the nimbus development.

title: Local Development Setup
description: Set up nimbus for local development
content:
Getting Started

Prerequisites

Before you begin, make sure you have the following installed:

- Node.js: v22.14.0+
- PNPM: v9.12.3+

Installation

1. Clone the repository:

``bash
git clone https://github.com/commercetools/nimbus.git
`

2. Enter the repository directory:

`bash
cd nimbus
`

3. Initialize the project:

`bash
pnpm nimbus:init
`

Development

Starting the Development Environment

Start the development environment with the following command:

`bash
pnpm start
`

This will start bot

title: Core Concepts
description: Quick start guide to set up and use the package.
content:
Core concepts

This page is designed to help you understand the foundational concepts so that
you can get quickly started with using the Nimbus Design System in your
projects.

Nimbus aims to provide a comprehensive suite of accessible and pre-themed React components, enabling developers to build beautiful and consistent user interfaces with ease. However, it's important to understand the following underlying concepts to make the most of the system.

Technical foundation

- Nimbus is a react com

title: Installation
description: How to add Nimbus to your react frontend
content:
Installation

How to add Nimbus to your react frontend.

1. Install the nimbus packages

You can install Nimbus using your preferred package manager.

Core Installation

Install the core Nimbus packages along with required peer dependencies:

pnpm

``bash
pnpm add @commercetools/nimbus @commercetools/nimbus-icons @chakra-ui/react
`

npm

`bash
npm install @commercetools/nimbus @commercetools/nimbus-icons @chakra-ui/react
`

yarn

`bash
yarn add @commercetools/nimbus @commercetools/nimbus-icons @

title: Release Process
description: How Nimbus releases work and how to stay informed
content:
Release Process

This page explains how Nimbus releases work, what to expect from each release
type, and how to stay informed about changes that may affect your application.

Release Cadence

Nimbus follows Semantic Versioning:

| Release Type      | Frequency    | Contents                           |
| ----------------- | ------------ | ---------------------------------- |
| Major (X.0.0) | As needed    | Breaking changes, major features   |
| Minor (x.Y.0) | As needed    | New features, non-br

title: Testing Setup
description: Configure Jest or Vitest to test Nimbus components
content:
Testing Setup

Nimbus components require specific polyfills to work in JSDOM test environments (Jest, Vitest). This guide shows you how to configure your test runner to work with Nimbus.

Why JSDOM Polyfills?

JSDOM is a headless browser environment used by Jest and Vitest. It doesn't include all browser APIs that Chakra UI and React Aria (which Nimbus is built on) depend on. Nimbus provides polyfills for these missing APIs.

The polyfills include:
- structuredClone - Required by Chakra UI for t

title: Patterns

content:
Patterns

A collection of reusable component patterns and compositions that combine multiple components to solve common UI challenges.

<CategoryOverview variant="list" />

title: Date range picker field
description: This pattern component integrates the DateRangePicker within a FormField using a simple, consistent, and opinionated API.
content:
Overview

DateRangePickerField is a pattern component that combines the FormField wrapper with the DateRangePicker input component. This integration provides a complete form field experience including accessible labels, helper text descriptions, info popovers for supplementary information, and comprehensive validation and error handling, all through a simplified, opinionated API.

Resources

For detailed guidance on the individual components that make up this pattern, consult the component guide

title: Fields
description: Field pattern components that integrate inputs within FormField for consistent form layouts
content:
Fields

Field pattern components provide a consistent way to integrate input components within FormField wrappers. These patterns simplify form building by combining labels, descriptions, validation, and error handling into a single, opinionated API.

When to use field patterns

Field patterns are ideal for:
- Building standard forms with consistent field layouts
- Reducing boilerplate in form code
- Ensuring accessibility best practices across all form fields
- Integrating with form libraries l

This integration provides a complete form field experience including accessible labels, helper text descriptions, info popovers for supplementary information, and comprehensive validation and error handling, all through a simplified, opinionated API.

The component contains two focusable inputs: a currency selector and an amount input field, providing an intuitive interface for mon

The component features increment and decrement buttons, keyboard navigation, locale-aware number formatting, and supports min/max

title: Spacing
description: Spacing tokens are used to define the space between elements.
content:
Spacing Tokens Spacing tokens are used to define the space between elements. They are used to create consistent spacing between elements and to ensure that the layout is visually balanced. > [!NOTE] > Every token has a negative equivalent, in case you need to apply a negative > margin etc. Access the negative version by prependind the token name with a > dash. e.g. -100, -400, -1000, etc.

> [!TIP] > To quickly calculate the token value in px, take the

title: Background
description: JSX style props for styling background colors, gradients, and images.
content:
Background

JSX style props for styling background colors, gradients, and images.

Background Attachment

Use bgAttachment to control the attachment of a background image.

``jsx
<Box bgAttachment="fixed" bgImage="url(...)" />
`

| Prop                                   | CSS Property            | Token Category |
| -------------------------------------- | ----------------------- | -------------- |
| bgAttachment, backgroundAttachment | background-attachment | -              |

Background Blend

Border

All sides

Use the rounded or borderRadius props to apply border radius on all sides of
an element.

``jsx
<Box borderRadius="400" />
<Box rounded="400" /> // shorthand
`

| Prop                      | CSS Property    | Token Category |
| ------------------------- | --------------- | -------------- |
| rounded, borderRadius | border-radius | radii        |

Specific sides

Use the rounded{Left,Right,Top,Bottom} or
bord

title: Display
description: Style props for styling display of an element.
content:
Display

Style props for styling display of an element.

Display Property

``jsx
<Box display="flex" />

// responsive
<Box display={{ base: "none", md: "block" }} />
`

| Prop      | CSS Property | Token Category |
| --------- | ------------ | -------------- |
| display | display    | -              |

Hiding Elements

Hide From

Use the hideFrom prop to hide an element from a specific breakpoint.

`jsx
<Box display="flex" hideFrom="md" />
`

| Prop       | CSS Property | Token Category |
| ---

title: Effects
description: JSX style props for styling box shadows, opacity, and more
content:
Effects

JSX style props for styling box shadows, opacity, and more

Box Shadow

Use the shadow or boxShadow prop to apply a box shadow to an element.

``jsx
// hardcoded values
<Box shadow="12px 12px 2px 1px rgba(0, 0, 255, .2)" />

// token values
<Box shadow="lg" />
`

| Prop                   | CSS Property     | Token Category |
| ---------------------- | ---------------- | -------------- |
| shadows, boxShadow | box-shadow     | shadows      |
| shadowColor          | --shadow-color | colo

title: Filters
description: JSX style props for applying various filters to elements.
content:
Filters

JSX style props for applying various filters to elements.

Filter

Use the filter prop to apply visual effects like blur or color shift to an
element.

``jsx
<Box filter="blur(5px)" />
<Box filter="grayscale(80%)" />
`

| Prop     | CSS Property | Token Category |
| -------- | ------------ | -------------- |
| filter | filter     | -              |

Blur

Use the blur prop to apply a blur effect to an element. The requirement for
this prop is to set the filter prop to auto.

`jsx
// har

title: Flex and Grid
description: JSX style props to control flex and grid layouts
content:
Flex and Grid

JSX style props to control flex and grid layouts

Flex Basis

Use the flexBasis prop to set the initial main size of a flex item.

``jsx
<Flex>
  <Box flexBasis="25%" />
  <Box flexBasis="25%" />
  <Box flexBasis="50%" />
</Flex>
`

| Prop        | CSS Property | Token Category |
| ----------- | ------------ | -------------- |
| flexBasis | flex-basis | -              |

Flex Direction

Use the flexDir or flexDirection prop to set the direction of the main axis
in a flex container

title: Interactivity
description: JSX style props to enhance interactivity on an element
content:
Interactivity

JSX style props to enhance interactivity on an element

Accent Color

Use the accentColor prop to set the accent color for browser generated
user-interface controls.

``jsx
// hardcoded
<label>
  <chakra.input type="checkbox" accentColor="#3b82f6" />
</label>

// token value
<label>
  <chakra.input type="checkbox" accentColor="blue.500" />
</label>
`

| Prop          | CSS Property   | Token Category |
| ------------- | -------------- | -------------- |
| accentColor | accent-colo

title: Layout
description: JSX style props to control the width, height, and spacing of elements
content:
Layout

JSX style props to control the width, height, and spacing of elements

Aspect Ratio

Use the aspectRatio prop to set the desired aspect ratio of an element.

``jsx
// raw value
<Box aspectRatio="1.2" />

// token
<Box aspectRatio="square" />
`

| Prop          | CSS Property   | Token Category |
| ------------- | -------------- | -------------- |
| aspectRatio | aspect-ratio | aspectRatios |

Break

Break After

Use the breakAfter prop to set how page, column, or region breaks should
beh

title: List
description: JSX style props for customizing list styles.
content:
List JSX style props for customizing list styles. List Style Type Use the listStyleType property to set the type of the list marker. ``jsx
  • Item 1
  • Item 2
  • Item 3
    •  ` | Prop | CSS Property | Token Category | | --------------- | ----------------- | -------------- | | listStyleType | list-style-type | - | List Style Position Use the listStylePosition property to set the position of the list

title: Sizing
description: JSX style props for sizing an element
content:
Sizing

JSX style props for sizing an element

Width

Use the width or w property to set the width of an element.

``jsx
// hardcoded values
<Box width="64px" />
<Box w="4rem" />

// token values
<Box width="5" />
<Box w="5" />
`

Fractional width

Use can set fractional widths using the width or w property.

Values can be within the following ranges:

- Thirds: 1/3 to 2/3
- Fourths: 1/4 to 3/4
- Fifths: 1/5 to 4/5
- Sixths: 1/6 to 5/6
- Twelfths: 1/12 to 11/12

`jsx
// half width
<Flex>
  <Box

title: Spacing
description: JSX style props for controlling the padding and margin of an element.
content:
Spacing

JSX style props for controlling the padding and margin of an element.

Padding

All sides

Use the padding prop to apply padding on all sides of an element

``jsx
// raw value
<Box padding="40px" />
<Box p="40px" /> // shorthand

// token value
<Box padding="4" />
<Box p="4" /> // shorthand
`

| Prop          | CSS Property | Token Category |
| ------------- | ------------ | -------------- |
| p,padding | padding    | spacing      |

Specific side

Use the padding{Left,Right,Top,Bottom}

title: SVG
description: JSX style props for SVG elements.
content:
SVG

JSX style props for SVG elements.

Fill

Use the fill prop to set the fill color of an SVG element.

``jsx
<chakra.svg fill="blue.500">
  <path d="..." />
</chakra.svg>
`

| Prop   | CSS Property | Token Category |
| ------ | ------------ | -------------- |
| fill | fill       | colors       |

Stroke

Use the stroke prop to set the stroke color of an SVG element.

`jsx
<chakra.svg stroke="blue.500">
  <path d="..." />
</chakra.svg>
`

| Prop     | CSS Property | Token Category |
| --------

title: Tables
description: JSX style props for styling table elements.
content: Tables JSX style props for styling table elements. Border Spacing Control the border-spacing property of a table. ``jsx Cell 1 Cell 2 ` | Prop | CSS Property | Token Category | | --------------- | ---------------- | -------------- | | borderSpacing | border-spacing | spacing | Border Spacing X Use the borderSpacingX prop to set the horizontal border-sp

title: Transforms
description: JSX style props for transforming elements.
content:
Transforms

JSX style props for transforming elements.

Scale

Use the scale prop to control the scale of an element.

``jsx
<Box scale="1.2" />
`

When the scale prop is set to auto, the scaleX and scaleY props are used
to control the scale of the element.

| Prop    | CSS Property | Token Category |
| ------- | ------------ | -------------- |
| scale | scale      | -              |

Scale X

Use the scaleX prop to control the scaleX property of an element. This
requires the scale prop to be se

title: Transitions
description: JSX style props for controlling an element's transition and animation.
content:
Transitions

JSX style props for controlling an element's transition and animation.

Transition

Use the transition prop to control the transition of an element.

``jsx
// hardcoded value
<Box bg="red.400" _hover={{ bg: "red.500" }} transition="background 0.2s ease-in-out">
  Hover me
</Box>

// shortcut value
<Box bg="red.400" _hover={{ bg: "red.500" }} transition="backgrounds">
  Hover me
</Box>
`

| Prop         | CSS Property | Token Category |
| ------------ | ------------ | --------------

title: Typography
description: JSX style props for styling text
content:
Typography

JSX style props for styling text

Font Family

Use the fontFamily prop to set the font family of a text element.

``jsx
<Text fontFamily="mono">Hello World</Text>
`

| Prop         | CSS Property  | Token Category |
| ------------ | ------------- | -------------- |
| fontFamily | font-family | fonts        |

Font Size

Use the fontSize prop to set the font size of a text element.

`jsx
// hardcoded values
<Text fontSize="12px">Hello World</Text>
<Text fontSize="10rem">Hello World</T

Animations

(shorthands) or creating custom animations or transitions by combining keyframe
animations with durations and easings.

Shorthands

The following list of shorthand tokens can be used as animation property
value, they combine a keyframe animation with duration and easing for a reusable
animation definition.

Borders

> [!IMPORTANT]

> included in the 100px, effectively reducing the amount of space available
> for content.

Proportional sizes

elements in layouts.

title: Typography
description: everything typography related
content:
Typography

Below you'll find the raw tokens to change typography styles on a text-element.

Text Styles

Text styles are collections of multiple typography rules (such as font-size,
line-height, letter-spacing etc.) that create a specific, reusable style.

> [!NOTE]  
> I added caption, detail & body from our existing styles, but they are
> essentially just combinations of font-size & line-height and can be mapped to
> xs, sm & md. The headline looks can be achieved by using the
> textStyles on

title: useHotkeys
description: use keyboard shortcuts to do stuff
content:
useHotkeys

This hook allows you to listen to hotkeys in a declarative way and execute a
callback function once the user pressed down the given hotkey.

Basic usage

Import the useHotkeys hook from the package:

``js
import { useHotkeys } from "@commercetools/nimbus";
`

Simple Hotkey

The most basic usage for the hook is to assign a hotkey you want to listen to
and a callback to get executed once the user hits that key.

`jsx live
import { useHotkeys } from "@commercetools/nimbus";

const App =
